# Tier 3 — Execution

The execution tier. Today: **Claude subagents** for in-ecosystem dogfooding; the cheap
path is **opencode + north-mini** via [`run-agent.sh`](../../run-agent.sh). Its seat in
the [Agent stack](../../AGENTS.md#agent-stack).

## Role
Turn implementation-instructions into code that passes the **local gate**. Make exactly
the change specified — no more. Loop implement → lint + focused tests → fix to green,
then commit. Escalate only on **non-convergence** (the failing set stops shrinking), with
a distilled report — never a transcript.

## Authors
- **the commits** (the diff) — the only artifact this tier owns

## Context budget — deliberately NARROW
This tier operationalizes the context-budget hypothesis: a narrow executor is
predicted to beat a fully-briefed one. Read **only**:

- its **implementation-instructions** (placed in the run worktree)
- the **touched files**
- those areas' **folder-scoped `GUIDELINES`**

**It does NOT read [`AGENTS.md`](../../AGENTS.md).** The loop doc — roles model, token
economy, milestone philosophy — is for the upper tiers; loading it here is plausibly
noise that dilutes focus. Everything this tier needs is in its implementation-instructions.

## Points at
- the **local-gate guidance** in [`.github/IMPL_INSTRUCTIONS_TEMPLATE.md`](../IMPL_INSTRUCTIONS_TEMPLATE.md)
  — converge to green (behaviour-checked, change-scoped) before pushing.
